summary: FPV drone operators with Russia's "Rubikon" Center intercepted over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with more than 70 enemy models destroyed, including 343 "Leleka-100" and 132 "Furia" drones. Ukrainian forces conducted deep strikes 50+ km into occupied Donetsk, targeting military and logistics vehicles, while Ukrainian company Vyriy developed new "Lightning" UAVs with retargeting capabilities. Russian forces reported that Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs are now carrying two FPV drones each, prompting calls for mobile fire teams to use frequency detectors and antidrone countermeasures. In Krasny Liman, optical fiber FPV drones "KVN" provided objective control footage of FAB-3000 strikes with UMPK hitting Ukrainian positions. Russian ZALA Lancet operators adapted to electronic warfare by timing strikes on grouped Ukrainian targets for maximum effect.
